title: PlatformConfigClient: Prevent thundering herd on cache expiry
body:
## Problem

When cache expires, multiple concurrent goroutines can trigger duplicate fetches, wasting API calls (thundering herd / cache stampede).

The check-then-act pattern between RLock check and fetch allows concurrent duplicate requests.

## Context

**Cache mutation issue was fixed in PR #321** - `FetchConfig` now returns deep copies of the config, preventing callers from corrupting the cached state.

This issue tracks the remaining thundering herd concern.

## Suggested Fixes

- Use `golang.org/x/sync/singleflight` for request coalescing
- Or use double-checked locking pattern where cache is checked again after acquiring write lock

## Impact

Low severity in practice - bop CLI typically runs single-threaded. This would matter more in MCP server context with concurrent requests.

## From Code Review

Findings:
- b7959c87229d8293872e0743bef54f09 (race condition)
- 9b0f348b8da0565a068f680bcef0e7ae (thundering herd)
